title: ARAM: Mayhem Augments - Complete List and Descriptions
text:
ARAM: Mayhem Augments - Complete List and Descriptions

The ARAM: Mayhem mode features three tiers of augments: Silver, Gold, and Prismatic. Silver augments include Adamant (Immobilizing or grounding an enemy champion grants 10 bonus armor and bonus magic resistance for 10 seconds, stacking up to 10 times), Deft (Grants 60% bonus attack speed), and Goredrink (Gain 15% omnivamp). Gold augments include Get Excited (Scoring a champion takedown grants you 100% bonus movement speed and 15% total attack speed for 4 seconds) and Executioner (Deal 10% increased damage to enemies below 30% of their maximum health. Scoring a takedown resets the cooldown of all basic abilities). Prismatic augments include Dashing (Abilities with dashes or blinks gain 175 ability haste) and Can't Touch This (Casting your ultimate grants you invulnerability for 2 seconds, 8 second cooldown).

title: Module:MayhemAugmentData/data - Structured Augment Data
text:
Module:MayhemAugmentData/data - Structured Augment Data

This Lua module contains structured data for all ARAM: Mayhem augments. Each augment entry includes: name, description (with wiki template markup), tier (Silver/Gold/Prismatic), and augment set. Notable entries include: Back to Basics (Gold) - Your champion abilities deal 35% increased damage and you gain 70 ability haste and 35% increased healing, but your ultimate ability is permanently sealed. Celestial Body (Prismatic) - Gain 1500 bonus health, but reduce your damage output by 10%. Dropkick (Prismatic) - Your basic attacks and abilities execute enemy champions below 5% of their maximum health.

title: Dashing augment on champions with dashes is absolutely broken in ARAM
text:
Dashing augment on champions with dashes is absolutely broken in ARAM

I just discovered that the Dashing augment (Prismatic) gives 175 ability haste to abilities with dashes or blinks. I tried it on Ezreal and his Arcane Shift (E) went from a 25 second cooldown to literally 3-4 seconds. Combined with Trinity Force and Ionian Boots, I was blinking every 2 seconds. Same thing on LeBlanc - her W cooldown becomes almost nonexistent. This augment is way overtuned for mobile champions. On champions like Yasuo it's also insane because his E dash gets the ability haste too. Definitely needs a nerf or should be limited to non-dash abilities only. Playing against an Ezreal with this augment in ARAM is a nightmare because you can never catch him.

title: ARAM: Mayhem Developer Update - Augment Design Philosophy
text:
ARAM: Mayhem Developer Update - Augment Design Philosophy

In this developer update, we want to share our design philosophy behind ARAM: Mayhem augments. Our goal was to create meaningful choices that change how you play your champion, not just stat sticks. Silver augments are designed to be solid picks that enhance your playstyle without being game-changing. Gold augments should feel impactful and create new synergies with your kit. Prismatic augments are meant to be game-defining picks that can fundamentally alter your champion's play pattern. We've been closely monitoring the balance of augments like Celestial Body, which grants 1500 bonus health but reduces damage output by 10%. Early data suggests it's performing well on tank champions but is correctly punished on damage dealers. We're also watching Executioner and Marked for Death, which share similar execute mechanics but serve different niches.

title: LoL ARAM Mayhem Tier List (2026): Best Augments To Pick
text:
LoL ARAM Mayhem Tier List (2026): Best Augments To Pick

Our ARAM Mayhem augment tier list ranks all augments by their impact on win rate and versatility. S-Tier augments include: Get Excited (Gold) - The takedown reset mechanic is incredibly strong in ARAM where teamfights are constant. One good kill can snowball into a pentakill with the movement speed and attack speed boost. Goliath (Prismatic) - 35% bonus health plus 15% adaptive force makes any champion a threat. The size increase also has psychological impact on enemies. B-Tier: Homeguard (Silver) - The 100% bonus movement speed is great for repositioning but falls off after the first hit. First-Aid Kit (Silver) - 20% heal and shield power is only useful on support champions. Avoid: Growth Spurt (Gold) - The knockup is unreliable and the health bonus doesn't compensate for the vulnerability during the animation.
